# Daniil Khremli

Senior Unity Developer with 9+ years of experience in Unity/C#, WebGL, multiplayer, Photon Fusion, Zenject, UniTask, Addressables, AR/VR and mobile development.

Primary CV website: https://iabloko.github.io/DaniilKhremli-CV/

## Main pages

- Main CV landing page: https://iabloko.github.io/DaniilKhremli-CV/
- English CV: https://iabloko.github.io/DaniilKhremli-CV/cv-en/index-en.html
- Russian CV: https://iabloko.github.io/DaniilKhremli-CV/cv-ru/index-ru.html
- Portfolio: https://daniilkhremli.notion.site/Khremli-Daniil-3558e0cfb75980f28465c7d441dc53a8?pvs=74
- Telegram: https://t.me/labloko
- Email: dxsunty@gmail.com

## Positioning

Daniil Khremli is a Senior Unity Developer focused on production Unity architecture, WebGL optimization, multiplayer gameplay systems, Photon Fusion, runtime content streaming, Addressables, SDK integrations and release preparation for browser/mobile platforms.

He has experience building Unity projects from scratch, owning client-side architecture, optimizing heavy WebGL runtimes, integrating backend APIs and shipping games to external platforms such as CrazyGames, Yandex Games and Telegram Mini Apps.

## Core expertise

- Unity/C#
- WebGL optimization
- WebGL memory and rendering optimization
- Runtime content streaming
- Chunk-based / cell-based world loading
- Photon Fusion / Photon PUN 2
- Multiplayer gameplay systems
- Network state synchronization
- Addressables and asset pipeline optimization
- Zenject / dependency injection
- UniTask / async workflows
- REST API / WebSocket integrations
- GPUI PRO / instancing optimization
- Binary data pipelines
- CrazyGames / Yandex Games / Telegram Mini Apps
- AR/VR and mobile development
- Team leadership, code reviews and mentoring

## Key achievements

- Designed and developed the client-side architecture of a WebGL UGC platform from scratch.
- Reduced WebGL browser tab memory usage by approximately 80%, from about 1.9 GB to about 400 MB.
- Reduced rendering load by approximately 40% through render pipeline optimization, batching/instancing, active object control and reducing unnecessary per-frame work.
- Improved initial WebGL loading by approximately 30%.
- Developed chunk/cell-based runtime world streaming to load and unload user-generated content by zones instead of keeping the full world in browser memory.
- Optimized client-side REST API communication by reducing redundant backend requests, improving data loading order, error handling, retry scenarios and networking stability.
- Optimized a large city-scale point cloud / instancing dataset from 1.5 GB to 300 MB.
- Improved city scene runtime performance from about 9 FPS to stable 60 FPS.
- Reduced CPU/GPU load for a city-scale WebGL instancing scene by roughly 90% and eliminated overheating on a mobile device.
- Migrated a production project from Photon PUN 2 to Photon Fusion 2.1.
- Led a team of 3 Unity developers through code reviews, coding standards, mentoring and codebase stabilization.

## Projects

### Megamod Games

WebGL UGC platform with multiplayer gameplay, user-generated worlds, runtime content loading, monetization and external platform releases.

Role: Senior Unity Developer / client-side architecture owner.

Impact:
- Designed and developed the client-side architecture of the UGC platform using Unity, Zenject and UniTask.
- Built gameplay systems, multiplayer integration, asset workflows and runtime loading of user-generated worlds.
- Reduced browser tab memory usage by approximately 80%, from about 1.9 GB to about 400 MB.
- Reduced rendering load by approximately 40%.
- Improved initial WebGL loading by approximately 30%.
- Developed chunk/cell-based runtime streaming for user-generated worlds.
- Optimized Addressables, asset lifecycle, loaded data ownership and runtime resource cleanup.
- Optimized REST API communication and improved client networking stability.
- Solved key UGC/WebGL bottlenecks: heavy user-generated scenes, browser memory pressure, slow initial loading, weak-device instability, runtime content streaming, resource cleanup and FPS degradation as object count increased.

Stack: Unity, WebGL, Photon Fusion, Zenject, UniTask, Addressables, REST API, WebSocket, Xsolla IAP, CrazyGames SDK, Yandex Games SDK.

Project link: https://www.crazygames.com/game/megamodgames

### San Francisco City Streaming

City-scale WebGL point cloud / GPUI PRO runtime streaming optimization project.

Role: Senior Unity Developer / owner of city streaming runtime architecture.

Impact:
- Converted a heavy monolithic point cloud / instancing dataset into a cell-based streaming system.
- Reduced the source point cloud dataset from 1.5 GB to 300 MB.
- Improved runtime performance from about 9 FPS to stable 60 FPS.
- Reduced CPU/GPU load by roughly 90%.
- Eliminated overheating on a mobile device.
- Implemented a production data pipeline: CSV source data -> binary chunks -> per-cell metadata -> runtime cell catalog -> GPUI batch upload.
- Loaded only required city areas around the player and unloaded inactive cells at runtime.
- Designed systems for runtime grid lookup, desired-cell planning, state tracking, controlled unload flow and batched GPUI uploads.

Stack: Unity, Mobile, Android, iOS, Addressables, GPUI PRO, binary data pipeline, runtime streaming, batching, object pooling.

### Pixel World

WebGL multiplayer shooter for Telegram Mini Apps.

Role: Senior Unity Developer.

Impact:
- Migrated and improved gameplay/network systems for movement, animation, shooting, state synchronization and weapon integration.
- Worked with Photon Fusion, Addressables, IAPs and WebGL runtime constraints.
- Prepared the project for CrazyGames publication and resolved platform-specific WebGL release issues.
- The project supports thousands of daily players, in-app purchases and shareable player statistics.

Stack: Unity, WebGL, Photon Fusion, Addressables, IAPs, gameplay/network systems.

Project link: https://pw.game/

### Portfolio

Collection of Unity projects, gameplay videos and production examples focused on WebGL, mobile, AR/VR, multiplayer gameplay, performance optimization and client architecture.

Portfolio link: https://daniilkhremli.notion.site/Khremli-Daniil-3558e0cfb75980f28465c7d441dc53a8?pvs=74

## Work experience summary

### Pixel World Games
Senior Unity Developer, November 2025 — April 2026.

Focus: Telegram Mini App, gameplay/network systems, Photon Fusion migration, WebGL release preparation.

### UNIBIT LLC / ONLYSPACE ONLINE GAMING SERVICES L.L.C
Senior Unity Developer, March 2022 — November 2025.

Focus: WebGL UGC platform, client-side architecture, Photon Fusion, Zenject, UniTask, Addressables, WebGL optimization, SDK integrations, external platform releases and team leadership.

### DSoneGames
Unity3D Developer, October 2020 — February 2022.

Focus: mobile hyper-casual games, SDK integrations, analytics and performance optimization for low-end devices.

### Ascreen
Junior Unity3D Developer, January 2017 — October 2020.

Focus: AR/VR B2B applications, Vuforia, Oculus Rift, HTC Vive, SteamVR, Oculus SDK and VRTK.

## Education

Master's degree, 2017.
Bonch-Bruevich Saint Petersburg State University of Telecommunications.
Radio Engineering Systems / Audiovisual Technology.
